items.title: Viewed at an oblique angle, the rendered image shows a disc-shaped assembly dominated by a large toothed ring...
items.condition:
Viewed at an oblique angle, the rendered image shows a disc-shaped assembly dominated by a large toothed ring at the outer boundary, three medium-sized spur gears evenly spaced inside it, and a flat three-lobed carrier linking them, with a cylindrical shaft extending axially. The grounded count is zero, and the tree depth is 1 (flat hierarchy with no sub-assemblies). The internal ring gear (ef30f3a4, 1 instance) measures 108 × 108 × 9.5 mm with an aspect ratio of 11.37, carries 60 internal teeth with conical flanks at ±20° inclination, and provides 3 cylinder-on-plane contacts where its outer rim seats against mating flat faces. Three 24-tooth spur gears (ef338bc2, 3 instances, each ~33.1 × 33.1 × 14.0 mm) serve as planet gears, each containing a central bore of ~7.7 mm diameter and six shallow blind recesses (diameter 22.5 mm, depth ~1.4–1.5 mm) forming hub regions; they contribute 3 mating-plane and 3 cylinder-on-plane contacts collectively. The tri-lobe carrier (ef333dca, 1 instance, ~63.8 × 57.5 × 6.0 mm thick) has three convex lobe tips at radius 25.0 mm blended by concave arcs at radius 8.5 mm, creating three pockets for the planet gears, and contributes 3 plane-on-plane contacts. Three solid cylindrical spacers (ef796e7a, 3 instances, diameter 12.5 mm × 5.5 mm tall) appear to serve as planet axle bushings or plugs seated in the carrier's lobe centers. Two straight bevel gears (ef5d820a and ef6b64ba, 1 instance each, ~29.79 × 29.79 × 8.87 mm, 24 teeth each) with conical tooth profiles and elliptical edge intersections appear to engage with the planet gears or provide input/output axes; one (ef6b64ba) has a blind bore of diameter 19.0 mm × 3.87 mm deep. Two plain cylindrical pins (ef7a58d8, diameter 11.0 mm × 49.0 mm; ef307ea4, diameter 10.0 mm × 49.0 mm) serve as shafts or locating dowels; the larger pin shows one plane-on-plane contact. Layout tags confirm a 3-fold rotational arrangement for both the planet gears and the spacers, both sharing an axis along [1, 0, 0].

Parts:
- gear (×3): A 24-tooth spur gear approximately 33.1 mm in outer diameter and 14 mm in face width (aspect ratio ≈ 2.37). Involute tooth profiles are represented by 288 B-spline surfaces with root fillets at 0.425 mm radius, while the root circle lies at radius 12.92 mm. A central bore of approximately 7.7 mm diameter appears to permit shaft mounting, and shallow 22.5 mm-diameter blind recesses on both axial faces create a raised hub. Three instances exist in the assembly, each participating in one plane-on-plane and one cylinder-on-plane contact, suggesting the gear likely seats axially against adjacent components while its bore engages a shaft.
- bevel_gear: A disc-shaped solid roughly 29.8 mm in diameter and 8.87 mm thick (aspect ratio 3.36) featuring 24 periodically spaced conical teeth, whose flanks are defined by 288 B-spline surfaces and whose root transitions carry small fillets at radius 0.3825 mm. A single Plane/Plane contact pair indicates one flat end face likely mates against an adjacent planar surface such as a shaft shoulder. The overall conical tooth geometry, elliptical edge profiles, and evenly distributed tooth-root fillets are consistent with a straight bevel gear that could transmit rotational motion between non-parallel shafts.
- bevel_gear: A straight bevel gear approximately 29.8 mm in outer diameter and 8.9 mm in face width (aspect ratio 3.36) with 24 teeth confirmed by the 24-fold periodicity of plane normals and 24 cylindrical root-segment faces at radius 11.63 mm. Tooth flanks are formed by 288 BSplineSurface faces on a conical pitch surface, and 144 elliptical edges arise from cone-cylinder intersections. A central blind bore of Ø 19.0 mm (depth 3.87 mm) along the gear axis, with a possible smaller Ø 8.0 mm pilot bore at its bottom, appears to permit shaft mounting. This part likely serves as a bevel gear that could mesh with a mating gear at an intersecting axis angle.
- cylindrical_spacer (×3): A plain solid cylinder 12.5 mm in diameter and 5.5 mm tall (aspect ratio ~2.27), comprising two flat end faces and one cylindrical lateral surface with a radius of 6.25 mm. It has no holes, chamfers, or internal features; its volume of ~675 mm³ matches a fully solid disc. Three instances exist in the assembly with no recorded contact pairs. The simple puck-like geometry suggests this part likely serves as a spacer, plug, or cap.
- internal_ring_gear: A thin annular ring (108 mm outer diameter, 9.5 mm thick, aspect ratio ~11.4) with approximately 60 internal teeth characterized by inclined planar flanks (±20° from radial), cylindrical root arcs at R = 41 mm, 122 conical tooth surfaces, and 120 small 0.625 mm fillets at tooth transitions. The smooth outer rim at R = 54 mm participates in 3 cylinder-on-plane assembly contacts, suggesting it seats in a flat-faced housing. This part likely serves as an internal ring gear or face gear that could mesh with a mating pinion or planet gears.
- pin: A simple solid cylinder approximately 11.0 mm in diameter (radius 5.5 mm) and 49.0 mm long, with an aspect ratio of about 4.5. It has just three faces — one cylindrical barrel and two flat circular end caps — with no holes, grooves, or stepped features. In the assembly it makes one plane-on-plane contact at an end face, suggesting it seats against a flat surface. The part likely serves as a locating pin or dowel, with its cylindrical surface permitting insertion into a matching bore.
- tri_lobe_knob: A thin three-lobed (tri-arm) solid knob approximately 64 mm across and 6 mm thick (aspect ratio ~10.6). Three convex lobe tips are formed by cylindrical faces of radius 25 mm, with concave inter-lobe blends at radius 8.5 mm, and chamfered perimeter edges (6 conical faces). It has two flat planar deck faces and no holes; in its assembly it participates in 3 plane-on-plane contacts via its flat surfaces. The ergonomic tri-lobe profile and flat mating faces suggest it likely serves as a hand-operated grip or adjustment knob.
- pin: A simple solid cylinder with a diameter of 10.0 mm (radius 5.0 mm) and an axial length of 49.0 mm, yielding an aspect ratio of approximately 4.9. The body has only three faces — one cylindrical outer surface and two flat, parallel end faces — with no holes, steps, or chamfers. No assembly contact pairs are recorded. Given its unadorned cylindrical form, this part likely serves as a dowel pin or locating pin that could be inserted into a matching bore.

Annotation Caveats:
No hole-edge inferences were detected despite the presence of bores on the planet gears (Ø7.7 mm) and bevel gear (Ø19 mm) and shaft pins (Ø10, Ø11 mm), suggesting clearance gaps or non-coincident placement in the captured configuration. The cylindrical spacers (ef796e7a) record no contact-surface pairs, so their precise seating location is inferred from the 3-fold layout tag rather than direct contact evidence. The pin ef307ea4 also shows no contacts. The two bevel gears are geometrically nearly identical (same bbox, same tooth count) but are stored as separate unique parts — whether they differ in bore configuration or tooth-hand is unclear from the data alone.
items.judge_reason: In views 2 and 3, PRED incorrectly features two parallel cylindrical shafts extending from the central sun gear area, whereas GT has only a single central shaft. In views 1 and 4, PRED's tri-lobe carrier has a circular recess/boss in the center, while GT's carrier has a smooth, solid center. Otherwise, the complex planetary gear arrangement, ring gear, and planet gears are well-replicated. The misplaced second shaft is a noticeable structural error.

items.title: Assembly Case · Linkage
items.condition:
Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly extends predominantly along one axis with a clearly planar, elongated profile — the bounding box spans 456.8 × 154.9 × 36.0 mm with an aspect ratio of 12.7. Two long flat mounting bars (200 × 38 × 4 mm each) run roughly parallel along the central spine, one with two Ø10 mm holes and one with two Ø6 mm holes, serving as the structural backbone. Four linkage arms (~163 × 4 mm, each with a 25 mm bore at one end and a 6 mm bore at the other) fan outward in symmetric pairs, their large bores fitting coaxially onto the protruding 25 mm-diameter hubs of four spur gears (Ø62 mm, 18 mm thick, ~60 teeth each, central Ø6 mm bore). These 4 coaxial cylindrical fits permit rotational engagement between the arms and gears. Four linear gear racks (~140 mm long, all-planar tooth geometry with ~20° flank angles and approximately 44–45 teeth) run lengthwise and mesh with the gears' peripheral teeth. Two featureless rectangular spacer plates (73.2 × 30.0 × 5.0 mm) bridge between the mounting bars and gears, contributing to the 27 mating-plane contacts. Thirteen washers (Ø15 mm, 2 mm thick, 6 mm bore) are distributed at pivot locations as axial spacers. The layout is classified as planar with 3 detected mirror pairs, consistent with the bilateral symmetry of the parallel-jaw geometry visible in the image. No fastener-through-hole stacks are detected in the assembly graph; the zero hole-edge count indicates pins or shafts are not modeled as separate bodies passing through holes, or the coaxial relationships are purely contact-based fits.

Parts:
- mounting_bar: A thin, flat rectangular bar measuring 200 × 38 × 4 mm (aspect ratio 50) with two 10 mm-diameter through-holes located near each end, passing through the 4 mm thickness. The part has 6 planar faces forming the prismatic body and 2 cylindrical bore surfaces. It participates in 4 plane-on-plane assembly contacts, suggesting its large flat faces mate flush against neighboring components. The end holes could each receive a bolt, and the part likely serves as a mounting bar or tie strap bridging two surfaces.
- washer (×13): A thin flat annular ring 15.0 mm in outer diameter with a 6.0 mm central through-hole and 2.0 mm thickness (aspect ratio 7.5). It has two smooth planar faces and featureless inner and outer cylindrical surfaces. The part appears in 13 instances throughout the assembly, interfacing solely via plane-on-plane contacts (17 total pairs), suggesting each flat face mates against an adjacent flat surface. The central bore permits passage of a fastener or pin of approximately 6 mm diameter, and the part likely serves as a flat washer or spacer distributing clamping load.
- gear (×2): A flat circular disk approximately 62 mm in diameter and 18 mm thick (aspect ratio 3.44) with 60 evenly spaced peripheral teeth whose tips lie at a radius of 31 mm, a central Ø 6 mm through-bore extending the full 18 mm axial length, and a Ø 25 mm hub boss protruding about 8 mm from one face. The part engages in 4 plane-on-plane and 2 cylinder-on-cylinder assembly contacts, indicating flat-face mating and a coaxial shaft fit through the bore. It appears to serve as a spur gear that mounts on a shaft via the central hole and meshes with another toothed component to transmit rotation.
- spur_gear (×2): A disk-shaped body approximately 62 mm in outer diameter and 18 mm thick (aspect ratio ~3.44) with 60 evenly spaced straight-cut teeth at a tip-circle radius of 31.0 mm. A central through-hole of 6.0 mm diameter spans the full thickness, and a cylindrical hub (diameter 25 mm, protruding ~8 mm) rises from one face. The part participates in 2 cylinder-on-cylinder and 4 plane-on-plane assembly contacts, with two instances present. This geometry appears to be a spur gear that could mount on a ~6 mm shaft through its bore and likely meshes with another toothed component via its peripheral teeth.
- linkage_arm (×4): A flat, elongated link approximately 163 × 81 × 4 mm with an aspect ratio of about 41:1, featuring rounded (stadium-profile) ends. One end contains a large 25 mm-diameter through-hole and the opposite end a small 6 mm-diameter through-hole, both passing through the full 4 mm thickness along the Z-axis. The part participates in 11 plane-on-plane and 4 cylinder-on-cylinder assembly contacts, and appears in 4 instances. It likely functions as a linkage arm or connecting lever, pivoting about the larger bore while the smaller bore could accept a pin or fastener.
- mounting_bar: A flat rectangular strip measuring 200 mm long × 38 mm wide × 4 mm thick (aspect ratio 50), with two 6 mm diameter through-holes positioned near each end, passing through the 4 mm thickness. The part contacts neighboring components via 4 plane-on-plane interfaces and is grounded in the assembly. It likely serves as a mounting bar or tie strip that could be bolted at both ends to flat mating surfaces.
- spacer_plate (×2): A plain rectangular slab measuring approximately 73.2 × 30.0 × 5.0 mm with an aspect ratio of 14.64, comprising 6 planar faces and 12 straight edges with no holes, bosses, or curved features. It participates in 4 plane-on-plane contacts with neighboring bodies and appears in 2 instances within the assembly. Given its featureless thin-plate form and multiple flat-face contacts, it likely serves as a spacer or filler plate held in position by surrounding components.
- gear_rack (×4): A long prismatic bar (aspect ratio ≈ 21.5, bbox 6.5 × 7.6 × 140 mm) with approximately 44–45 repeating trapezoidal teeth along one edge, all composed of planar faces and straight edges. The tooth flanks are inclined at roughly 20° from the vertical, as indicated by the angled plane normals (±0.94, 0.342, 0). The flat bottom face participates in 4 Plane-on-Plane contacts in the assembly, and the part appears in 4 grounded instances. This part likely serves as a linear gear rack whose tooth flanks could mesh with a mating pinion to permit linear-to-rotary motion conversion.

Annotation Caveats:
No fastener or pin bodies are modeled through any of the numerous through-holes (hole_edge_count = 0), so the coaxial engagement between linkage-arm bores and gear hubs is inferred solely from 4 cylinder-on-cylinder contact pairs rather than from fastener-through-hole stacks. The two gear types (part_id 3ab4793a and 3ab56386) have nearly identical geometry — both Ø62 mm, 60 teeth, 18 mm thick — and differ only in internal part ID, making their distinct roles ambiguous. All 29 instances are grounded, which may reflect a single-configuration snapshot rather than true kinematic constraints.
items.judge_reason: PRED and GT both represent a planar linkage assembly with gears, gear racks, linkage arms, and central mounting bars. The primary geometric difference is in the central structure: GT features two distinct spacer plates separating the upper and lower mounting bars, located near the gear assemblies. PRED instead has a single, large central block bridging the mounting bars. Additionally, the positioning and length of the central mounting bars relative to the gears differ slightly. PRED maintains high aesthetic quality with modeled gear teeth, rack teeth, and distinct components.

items.title: In the rendered image the assembly stands vertically, roughly 800 mm tall and 277 mm across, with a...
items.condition:
In the rendered image the assembly stands vertically, roughly 800 mm tall and 277 mm across, with a flat disc plate at the top, a long thin rod descending from it, and a barrel-shaped cage of slats at the lower end. The grounded part is the disc plate (180 mm diameter × 10 mm thick, one central 10 mm through-hole), which serves as the topmost anchor. The rod is a featureless solid cylinder of 10 mm diameter and 500 mm length (aspect ratio 50) that passes through the disc plate's central bore; hole inference confirms this link with zero perpendicular misalignment and 0.3 mm axial overlap. At the lower end of the rod, a flanged bushing (56.5 mm outer flange diameter, 40 mm through-bore, 11.25 mm tall) and a bushing/locating plug (20 mm main-body radius, 23 mm rim radius, 77 mm tall with a conical lower taper and a blind 24 mm bore at the top) are linked coaxially through a second hole inference edge (diameter 40 mm, zero perpendicular misalignment, 11.85 mm axial overlap matching the bushing's full height). These hub components sit at the top of the cage. A splined ring (200 mm diameter, 3.9 mm thick, 40 external teeth, inner bore ~160 mm) encircles the hub zone; its 40 teeth match the 40 vane slats in count. Each vane slat is an extremely thin panel (~3.9 × 50 × 300 mm, aspect ratio 76.9) with a gentle outward curvature (cylinder radius 572.5 mm on one face) and two edge notches that likely engage with the splined ring's teeth for location and retention. A layout tag confirms a 40-fold rotational arrangement of the slats about the Y-axis, centered at approximately y = −80.4 mm. Completing the assembly is a knob (60 mm wide, 81 mm tall) with a spherical upper dome and conical stem that appears to cap the hub. One part (3f1c184a, single instance) lacks geometric data and may serve as an additional connector or spacer in the hub region.

The overall proportions and assembly are mostly correct, but the slat shape differences are significant. Aesthetics are high as the model is clean and well-structured, despite the geometric deviations.

In the rendered image the assembly presents as an elongated flat board elevated on a pair of crossed tubular legs, with horizontal foot bars at the base. The grounded part is the flat board panel (f871a2ac, 1 instance), an asymmetric oblong slab approximately 1576 × 500 × 30 mm with a full-perimeter bullnose edge at R = 15 mm. Nesting onto this board is a cover panel (f86e6e40, 1 instance, ~1624 × 515 × 31 mm) whose stepped perimeter rim (outer R ≈ 15.45 mm, inner R ≈ 15.15 mm) seats into or over the board's rounded edge. Beneath the board, a mounting plate (f8743ab4, 1 instance, ~500 × 150 × 50 mm) with five ∅20 mm through-holes — three perpendicular to the plate face and two at oblique angles — serves as the hinge bracket connecting the legs to the board underside; the oblique hole axes (~[0.74, −0.67, 0] and ~[0.61, −0.79, 0]) match the splay angles of the crossed legs. A smaller mounting bar (f87a06f8, 1 instance, ~377 mm long) with three downward-projecting bosses provides a secondary attachment point. The leg structure consists of a Y-shaped tubular brace frame (f875c13a, 1 instance, tubes at R = 10 mm, ~600 × 936 × 1012 mm bbox) and a T-shaped tubular frame (f8791cfa, 1 instance, tubes at R = 10 mm, ~1360 × 600 × 20 mm bbox). These two frames cross and are joined by a single stepped pin (f87091a4, 1 instance, ∅10 mm shaft with ∅15 mm shoulder heads, 78 mm long) that passes through ∅10.183 mm through-holes in each frame; both hole-edge links show zero perpendicular offset and ~20.6 mm axial overlap, indicating a tight coaxial fit that permits rotation or sliding along the pin axis. Six hex nut fasteners (f870e00a, ~M10 bore, 4 mm thick) and two grooved retention pins (f876848c, fluted shank ~42 mm long with domed heads) complete the fastener inventory. Six additional part instances (f876d310) are present but lack geometry data. The layout tag confirms a single mirror pair, consistent with the bilateral symmetry of the leg arrangement about the board's longitudinal centreline.

The mounting plate's five ∅20 mm through-holes appear geometrically compatible with the ∅20 mm tubes but no hole-edge links were generated for them, possibly because the tube ends are modeled as solid cylinders rather than canonical fastener bodies. The T-frame's two ∅8 mm holes have no matching fastener body in the assembly. The cover panel's interface with the board panel is inferred from matching outlines and rim geometry but is not confirmed by any contact or hole evidence.
items.judge_reason: PRED and GT both represent an ironing board, sharing the same semantic category. However, there are significant geometric differences, particularly on the underside. In GT, the legs attach to brackets located near both ends of the board, and there is a central mounting bar with three distinct cleats. In PRED, the T-frame leg attaches to a long, simplistic block in the middle of the board rather than at the rear end, leaving the rear underside bare and changing the structural stance. Additionally, the GT board features a smoothly curved taper towards the pointed end, whereas the PRED board uses straight angled edges that meet at a blunter tip. The PRED model is nonetheless clean and recognizable as an engineered object, retaining good visual quality despite the structural inaccuracies.

items.title: Viewed in the render, the assembly presents an elongated, stadium-ended enclosure oriented along its longest axis (~204 mm),...
items.condition:
Viewed in the render, the assembly presents an elongated, stadium-ended enclosure oriented along its longest axis (~204 mm), with a flat top deck populated by circular button pockets of at least three distinct diameters and a rectangular recess near the center. The body is roughly 204 × 67 × 36 mm overall (aspect ratio ~5.63) and divides into two primary structural halves: the upper housing panel (part 16d7b822, 1 instance, ~204 × 66 × 14 mm, 76 B-spline + 29 planar + 19 cylindrical faces) and the lower housing tray (part 170bc078, 1 instance, ~202 × 67 × 22 mm, 41 B-spline + 23 planar + 4 cylindrical faces). Both halves are grounded and engage each other through 14 freeform-on-freeform contacts along the sculpted perimeter walls and multiple mating-plane contacts along the rim and parting line.

Button caps dominate the instance count: 10 identical large caps (part 173f2cba, radius 6.35 mm, 12.7 mm diameter, 3.81 mm tall with toroidal top fillet), 2 medium caps (part 173fa200, radius 3.715 mm), and three single-instance caps at radii 3.74, 3.93, and 6.47 mm (parts 173ff02c, 17403e40, 1742fd48). Each cap mates via a flat bottom Plane/Plane contact into a corresponding blind recess on the upper panel. Two retaining pins with slotted conical flared bases (parts 16d40e9c and 16d4842e, shaft radii ~1.22 and ~1.39 mm, lengths ~29 and ~26 mm respectively) each participate in 2 coaxial cylindrical fits, passing through aligned bores in the two housing halves—accounting for all 4 coaxial-fit contacts. Two thin, lenticular fairing elements (parts 16d4aade and 17046d7e, ~16 × 6 × 0.9 mm, aspect ratio ~19) and two blade-like elements (parts 17077ab6 and 17099d94, ~16 × 4 × 0.8 mm, aspect ratio ~20) nest into the shell via mixed freeform-on-plane and freeform-on-freeform contacts. A rectangular spacer bar (part 1742af24, ~3.8 × 11.4 × 33.7 mm with 0.5 mm edge fillets) seats in the rectangular window recess via one Plane/Plane contact. A filler insert (part 173ede78, elliptical planform ~2 × 8 mm) and a large reference surface panel (part 16d7910c, open shell ~229 × 127 mm, no contacts) complete the inventory. The layout-tag analysis identifies one mirror pair, consistent with the two pins or the two fairing/blade element sets being reflections of each other across the long axis.

- pin: A slender cylindrical pin approximately 29 mm long with a shaft radius of about 1.22 mm (aspect ratio ≈ 7.6). The bottom end flares outward via two conical faces that are longitudinally slotted by four radial planar cuts, creating a multi-pronged base. The shaft engages two coaxial cylinder-on-cylinder contacts with neighbouring parts, and the conical flare seats against one NURBS surface. This part likely serves as a retaining or expanding pin whose split flared end could permit insertion and elastic retention within aligned bores.
- retaining_pin: A slender cylindrical pin approximately 26.4 mm long with a shaft radius of about 1.39 mm (diameter ≈ 2.79 mm) and an aspect ratio of 6.8. The shaft terminates at one end in a flared, slotted conical base (two conical faces and six radial planar cut faces forming flexible tabs) that widens to roughly 3.96 mm across. The part has no holes and contacts two cylindrical bores via its shaft. It likely serves as a retaining or snap-fit pin whose slotted flared base could permit insertion through a hole and then resist withdrawal.
- fairing_cap: A very thin, elongated lenticular solid approximately 16.3 × 6.2 × 0.86 mm with an aspect ratio of about 18.9, composed of one flat bottom face (normal [0,1,0]) and two freeform B-spline surfaces forming a smooth convex upper profile. It has no holes or cylindrical features. items.title: In the rendered image the assembly appears as a rectangular box roughly 212 × 102 × 158 mm...
items.condition:
In the rendered image the assembly appears as a rectangular box roughly 212 × 102 × 158 mm on an L-shaped bracket that forms both a vertical back wall and a horizontal base plate; the front face shows two large circular openings flanking a recessed rectangular panel bearing raised symbol shapes, and the top carries parallel longitudinal ribs. The grounded parts are the two disc-shaped caps (each ~55 mm diameter, conical rim with a toroidal fillet, ~1.4 mm effective thickness). The main enclosure body is the link_block (1 instance, ~58 × 92 × 210 mm), an elongated prismatic block with two full-length stepped bores (ø6 and ø3 mm) and 19 planar faces; it engages 11 plane-on-plane contacts and 2 cylinder-on-elliptical-cylinder contacts. The L-bracket panel (1 instance, ~86 × 87 × 210 mm) carries two perpendicular thin plates joined by a 1 mm radius bend fillet and two shallow ø57 mm blind recesses (0.5 mm deep), contributing 3 planar contacts. The front mounting plate (1 instance, ~210 × 85 × 3 mm) has two counterbored ø55 mm through-holes with bolt-circle features at ø3.07 and ø2.67 mm, yielding 12 planar contacts and 4 coaxial fits. Two flanged bell housings (~55 × 55 × 28 mm each) seat into the front bores; each is axisymmetric with a stepped cylindrical barrel (radii 20–27.5 mm), a B-spline bell interior with a spherical dome, toroidal fillet transitions, and a thin flange (0.635 mm) carrying six ø2.671 mm through-holes; they register through 4 coaxial fits and 2 planar contacts but have no modeled fastener bodies. Two spacer rings (ø57/ø55 mm, 2.5 mm thick, 1 mm wall) sit coaxially around the housings, each contributing 2 planar contacts. The top surface is built from a trapezoidal cover panel (210 × 84 mm, rounded corners at R 1.5 mm), six brace strips (1.2 × 12.2 × 78.5 mm, aspect ratio 65, one mitered end), and three flat strips (1.6 × 4.1 × 190 mm, aspect ratio 118, double-angled ends). The raised-symbol panel zone contains a rectangular beveled frame bezel (1 × 37 × 77 mm), a cross-shaped connector (12 × 12 × 2 mm), a C-shaped retaining ring (outer R 6.5, inner R 4.5 mm), two elongated keys with rounded ends (83.5 × 3.1 × 1.2 mm), a square key (2 × 2 × 12 mm), a rounded-end key tab (2 × 3 × 10.7 mm), and an angular bracket plate (2 × 7.5 × 14.7 mm with cutouts). The 33 contacts decompose into 24 mating planes, 6 coaxial fits, 3 elliptical-cylinder/cylinder contacts, and 3 ambiguous edges; layout tags confirm a planar arrangement with 4 mirror pairs reflecting bilateral symmetry.

Parts:
- flanged_bell_housing (×2): A bell-shaped body of revolution roughly 55 mm in diameter and 28 mm deep (aspect ratio ≈ 1.95), featuring a thin outer flange with six evenly spaced through-holes (diameter ≈ 2.67 mm) and a cylindrical barrel section (R = 20 mm, ≈ 11.7 mm long) at its narrower end. The interior transitions through smooth B-spline and spherical surfaces into a dome-shaped cavity. Assembly contacts include 2 plane-on-plane flange matings, 4 cylinder-on-cylinder coaxial fits, and 2 cylinder-on-elliptical-cylinder interfaces; two instances of this part are present. The bolt pattern and flanged form suggest this part likely serves as a bell-mouth housing or nozzle that mounts flush against a receiving surface.
- mounting_plate: A thin rectangular plate approximately 210 × 85 × 3 mm (aspect ratio ~69) featuring two large counterbored through-holes of 55 mm diameter spaced along the long axis, each surrounded by a circular pattern of small fastener holes (d ≈ 3 mm). The plate participates in 12 plane-on-plane and 4 cylinder-on-cylinder assembly contacts, indicating it mates flat against adjacent structure while receiving cylindrical components through its bores. This geometry suggests the part likely serves as a mounting plate or bulkhead that permits two cylindrical elements to pass through and be secured by bolt-circle flanges.
- cover_panel: A thin, elongated, roughly trapezoidal flat panel measuring approximately 210 × 84 mm in plan with an estimated through-thickness of about 1.5 mm (aspect ratio ≈ 20.63). Its outline features four small rounded corners (cylinder radius 1.5 mm) and B-spline edge profiles that include an angled cut at one end. The part has no holes and participates in a single plane-on-plane contact, suggesting it likely mates flat against an adjacent component. It appears to serve as a cover panel or partition retained by non-bolted means.
- link_block: An elongated prismatic block approximately 210 mm long with a 3.63 aspect ratio, featuring two stepped through-bores (diameters 6.0 mm and 3.0 mm) running along its full length, with slightly angled faces (~6° taper) on one or more sides. It participates in 11 plane-on-plane contacts and 2 cylinder-on-elliptical-cylinder contacts, indicating extensive flat mating plus pin-type bore engagements. The geometry suggests this part likely serves as a structural connecting link or block, transmitting loads between two pin joints while seating against flat neighboring surfaces.
- key: An elongated flat solid (aspect ratio ~5.3, bbox 2.0 × 3.0 × 10.67) with semicircular rounded ends formed by cylindrical surfaces at radii 4.5 and 1.0, eight planar faces forming the broad flat sides and a protruding lateral tab near mid-height, and no holes. It participates in a single plane-on-plane contact in its assembly. The overall profile — flat, rounded at both ends, with a lateral engagement tab — suggests this part likely serves as a retaining key or latch element that could slide into a mating slot.
- retaining_ring: An open C-shaped ring with an outer radius of 6.5 mm, inner radius of 4.5 mm, and a 2.0 mm square cross-section, producing a flat arc with semicylindrical rounded tips (radius 1.0 mm) at its open gap. The part has only two flat faces (top and bottom, normals ±Z) and participates in one Plane/Plane contact with a neighbouring component. Its geometry — a split ring with no holes and smooth ends — suggests it likely serves as a retaining ring or snap clip that could encircle a shaft of approximately 9 mm diameter and bear axially against a flat mating surface.
- bracket_plate: A thin, flat angular plate with a complex polygonal outline, measuring roughly 2.0 thick and up to about 14.7 long (aspect ratio ~7.4). All 23 faces are planar with straight edges only; the outline includes segments at oblique angles (~42° and ~132° from one axis) that form perpendicular pairs, producing a zigzag profile with a forked lower region. Two rectangular cutouts pass through the broad face and could permit tab-and-slot interlocking with mating flat parts. The body has no holes and participates in a single plane-on-plane contact, so it likely serves as a structural bracket or gusset plate providing angular support within the assembly.
- cross_connector: A flat, uniform-thickness plus-sign (cross) extrusion with a bounding box of approximately 2.0 × 12.0 × 12.0 and an aspect ratio of 6.0. All 14 faces are planar and all 36 edges are straight lines, forming four orthogonal arms each 12.0 long and 2.0 wide that share a central 2.0 × 2.0 overlap. No holes or curved features are present. The part participates in one Plane-on-Plane contact in the assembly, suggesting it mates flatly against a neighboring component. It appears to serve as a structural cross-piece or interlocking connector element.
- key: A solid rectangular bar with a square cross-section (2 × 2) and a length of 12, yielding an aspect ratio of 6.0. The geometry consists of exactly 6 planar faces and 12 straight edges with no holes, fillets, or curved surfaces. It participates in a single Plane/Plane contact in the assembly, suggesting one flat face mates against a receiving surface. The square-section elongated form and single flat contact are consistent with a parallel key that could sit in a keyway to permit torque transfer between mating components.
- frame: A very thin (1 mm thick) rectangular frame measuring approximately 37 × 76.5 mm in plan, with an aspect ratio of 76.5 and a large central rectangular opening that removes most of the plate area. All 10 faces are planar and all 24 edges are straight lines; four perimeter walls are beveled at 45° between the front and back faces. The part has no holes or cylindrical features and participates in one plane-on-plane contact with an adjacent body. It likely serves as a retaining frame, spacer, or bezel that mates flat against another surface to surround or locate a component within its central opening.
- flat_strip (×3): A very elongated flat prismatic strip with a rectangular cross-section of roughly 4.1 mm × 1.6 mm and a bounding-box length of 190 mm, giving an aspect ratio of about 118:1. All six faces are planar and all twelve edges are straight lines; the two end faces are cut at steep angles, producing pointed tips. The part has no holes or curved features and participates in three plane-on-plane contacts in the assembly, with three instances present. It likely serves as a spacer strip or shim, mating flat against neighbouring components.
- brace_strip (×6): A very thin, elongated flat strip approximately 1.2 × 12.19 × 78.52 mm with an aspect ratio of about 65.4. All six faces are planar and all twelve edges are straight; one end appears square-cut while the other is mitered at roughly 45°. The part has no holes or curved features and participates in 6 plane-on-plane contacts in the assembly. Six identical instances exist, and the mitered end combined with the extreme slenderness suggests the part likely serves as an angled brace or structural strip connecting flat surfaces at an angle.
- spacer_ring (×2): A thin annular ring with an outer diameter of 57 mm, an inner bore diameter of 55 mm, and an axial thickness of 2.5 mm, yielding a radial wall thickness of only 1.0 mm and an aspect ratio of 22.8. It consists of two flat annular faces and two concentric cylindrical surfaces. In the assembly it participates in 2 plane-on-plane contacts and appears in 2 instances, suggesting both flat faces seat against adjacent components. The part likely serves as a spacer or shim ring positioned between two coaxial flat-faced surfaces.
- l_bracket_panel: A thin-walled L-shaped bracket roughly 210 mm long with two perpendicular flat plates (~86 × 87 mm cross-section, aspect ratio 2.436) joined by a 1.0 mm-radius bend fillet. The horizontal plate contains two shallow blind circular recesses of 57.0 mm diameter and ~0.5 mm depth, arranged diagonally. Edges are finished with small fillets (0.25–0.5 mm radii), and complex B-spline blend surfaces appear at the corners. The part participates in three Plane-on-Plane assembly contacts, suggesting it mates flatly against neighboring components. It likely serves as a mounting bracket or partition panel, with the two shallow circular pockets possibly acting as locating seats for cylindrical elements.
- key (×2): A very slender flat rectangular bar approximately 83.5 mm long with a cross-section of roughly 3.1 × 1.2 mm (aspect ratio 68.25), featuring 4 planar faces and 2 cylindrical end-cap surfaces of radius ~3.1 mm. It has no holes and contacts mating parts through 2 plane-on-plane interfaces. Two instances exist in the assembly, and the part is not grounded. The overall geometry—a simple prismatic strip with rounded ends and flat mating faces—suggests this part likely serves as a parallel key or positioning element seated in a keyway groove.
- cap (×2): A thin disc approximately 55 mm in diameter with an aspect ratio of 7.83, featuring two flat parallel faces, a conical bevel around the outer rim, and a toroidal fillet at one transition. No holes or cylinder surfaces are present. The calculated average thickness is about 1.4 mm, with the raised rim spanning the 7.03 mm axial extent. With two instances in the assembly and a simple axisymmetric profile, this part likely serves as a cap or cover that could seat into or over a cylindrical opening.

Annotation Caveats:
Three ambiguous contact edges remain unclassified. Despite 12 through-holes on the two flanged bell housings and bolt-circle features on the mounting plate, hole_edge_count is 0, indicating no fastener bodies are modeled in the assembly. The link_block's two long stepped bores were not captured by the hole-detection algorithm (holes_on_part empty), likely due to angled end faces and stepped geometry. The L-bracket panel's two blind ø57 mm recesses (0.5 mm deep) likewise do not appear in hole_edges_summary. Grounding is assigned to the two cap instances rather than to the L-bracket, which may reflect an unconventional coordinate-system choice.
items.judge_reason: View 1: PRED's base plate features raised crescent arcs, whereas GT has shallow circular recesses. PRED's front circular features include 6 small dots (holes/pegs) which are absent in GT. The central symbols also differ slightly. View 2: Both models share the overall boxy shape, top ribs, and side ribs. View 3 & 4: PRED's bottom surface is completely flat, whereas GT has four distinct circular feet. Overall, PRED captures the main enclosure design well but misses the bottom feet and misinterprets the base plate recesses and front circular details.

items.title: Viewed in the rendered image, a long, flat rectangular strip extends diagonally, its top surface populated with a...
items.condition:
Viewed in the rendered image, a long, flat rectangular strip extends diagonally, its top surface populated with a repeating pattern of square housings, small rectangular blocks, and thin frame elements. The assembly's bounding box measures 50.0 × 9.5 × 1.7, yielding an aspect ratio of roughly 29.4, confirming the ribbon-like form. The single grounded connection strip (part 43a9f262, 1 instance) spans the full 50 mm length and carries 476 B-spline faces corresponding to the embossed text and symbols visible at both ends, along with 4 cylindrical faces at radius ~1.347 forming the semicircular end notches. Seated on this strip are 3 housing blocks (2 instances of part 43b7ae54 plus 1 instance of housing_base 43b1bab6), each roughly 5.1 × 4.7 × 1.3 with a central circular B-spline recess and protruding side tabs; these are the prominent square pads with dished centers visible at regular intervals. Two D-shaped retainer plates (part 43b0d036, each ~2.7 × 2.3 × 0.4) carry blind round holes of diameter ~1.664 and depth 0.1, oriented along the Y-axis; these mate via planar and coaxial contacts. The 3 angle strips, 3 pentagonal frame shims, and 3 U-shaped frame strips are all extremely thin (~0.001 thick) planar elements sitting on the strip deck, visible as the small rectangular and outlined features between the housings. Two additional part classes (43b2a54a ×3 and 43b11e74 ×2) lack geometric extraction but account for some of the small raised blocks visible in the image. Contact evidence totals 23 mating-plane pairs and 4 coaxial-fit pairs, with zero fastener-through-hole stacks. Layout analysis confirms a linear chain along the X-axis and 3 mirror pairs reflecting the left-right symmetry of components about the strip's center.

Parts:
- connection_strip: A very thin, flat, elongated strip (aspect ratio ~124.7, roughly 0.4 mm thick × 9.5 mm wide × 50 mm long) with embossed relief text at both ends represented by 476 B-spline surfaces. Each short end features a pair of open semi-circular notches at radius 1.35 mm, which participate in 4 cylinder-on-cylinder contacts with neighboring components. The broad flat faces engage in 23 plane-on-plane contacts, indicating extensive flat mating. This part likely serves as a bridging connection strip that spans between two adjacent components, with the end notches permitting it to seat around cylindrical pins or posts.
- retainer_plate (×2): A thin D-shaped plate (aspect ratio ~6.74, approximately 2.69 × 2.35 × 0.4) with a semicircular outer edge at radius ~1.35 and a flat truncation on one side. Each broad face carries a shallow blind circular recess of diameter ~1.66 and depth 0.1, whose cylindrical walls participate in 2 cylinder-on-cylinder assembly contacts, indicating cylindrical components seat within. Four plane-on-plane contacts involve the top, bottom, and flat-edge faces. The flat edge likely serves as an anti-rotation key, and the part appears to function as a retainer plate or locating washer that constrains and positions cylindrical features.
- part (×2): 
- housing_base: A flat, nearly square block (approximately 5.1 × 4.7 × 1.3 mm, aspect ratio ~3.9) featuring a concentric circular recess formed by BSpline surfaces on the top face and 45° chamfers at the upper corners. Small rectangular tabs protrude from the lower sides, and rectangular notches separate them, creating a comb-like lower profile. The part participates in a single Plane/Plane contact on its bottom face and has no holes. It appears to function as a housing base or component seat, with the top recess likely receiving a circular mating element and the side tabs providing alignment or retention.
- part (×3): 
- housing (×2): A thin, nearly square block (roughly 5.1 × 4.7 × 1.3 mm, aspect ratio ≈ 3.9) featuring a central circular recess formed by freeform (BSpline) surfaces rather than cylindrical walls, and a 45° corner chamfer. Rectangular tabs protrude from two adjacent sides, likely serving as alignment or retention features. The part has no holes and participates in 2 plane-on-plane contacts in the assembly. With 2 instances present and its shallow dished pocket, it appears to function as a small housing or seat that could locate a round component while mating to flat surfaces of neighboring parts.
- angle_strip (×3): A very thin flat L-shaped strip with two straight arms meeting at roughly 90 degrees, having a thickness of about 0.001 units and an overall extent of approximately 2.4 × 3.3, yielding an aspect ratio near 3276. All 10 faces are planar and all 24 edges are straight lines, with no holes or curved features. It participates in 3 plane-on-plane contacts and appears in 3 instances within the assembly. This part likely serves as a thin angle shim, edge trim, or flat sealing strip positioned along a corner where two planar surfaces meet.
- frame_or_shim (×3): A very thin pentagonal frame with a large central opening, having bounding-box extents of roughly 8.51 × 6.67 × 0.001 and an extreme aspect ratio of about 8512. All 12 faces are flat planes and all 30 edges are straight lines, with no holes or curved features. Three instances exist in the assembly, participating in 3 plane-on-plane contacts via its broad flat faces. The part likely serves as a thin shim, spacer, or gasket frame interposed between flat mating surfaces, with the pointed end of the pentagon potentially aiding orientation.
- frame_strip (×3): A very thin, flat strip (aspect ratio ~3268:1, bbox ~3.27 × 2.40 × 0.001) forming three sides of an open rectangle, with 10 planar faces and 24 straight edges — no holes or curved surfaces. It participates in 3 plane-on-plane contacts and appears in three instances within the assembly. The part likely serves as a frame strip or border element that mates flush against adjacent flat surfaces.

Annotation Caveats:
Two part classes (43b2a54a ×3 instances, 43b11e74 ×2 instances) have null extraction status and no geometric descriptions, so their shapes are inferred only from the rendered image. The 3 toroidal surfaces in the global histogram are unattributed to any extracted part. Blind holes on the retainer plates (diameter ~1.664) have no corresponding fastener bodies and appear in no hole_edges_summary clusters, consistent with them being blind recesses only.
items.judge_reason: Both models represent an LED strip or similar electronic ribbon component with square housings and small rectangular surface blocks. PRED accurately captures the layout of the housings, the central circular recesses, the small intermediate blocks, and even the embossed text ('24V') on the top surface. However, there is a prominent geometric difference at the ends of the strip: PRED features semi-circular cutouts with D-shaped rings protruding outward past the main rectangular footprint, altering the overall silhouette. In contrast, GT has perfectly straight, flat rectangular ends with the circular features contained entirely within the strip's boundaries. Aside from the ends, the proportions and feature details match very well. PRED is highly detailed and cleanly modeled.

items.title: Assembly Case · Barrel Cradle
items.condition:
Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents a horizontally oriented barrel sitting in a low rectangular cradle. The barrel's body is the dominant structure: 18 identical curved segments (cd83668a, each ~33 × 33.5 × 259.6 mm, aspect ratio 7.875) tile together in an 18-fold rotational pattern about the barrel's long axis (layout tag: n_fold_rotation, n = 18, axis [0, 0, −1]). Each stave has B-spline-dominated curvature and stepped tongue-and-groove end features defined by small-radius cylindrical patches (R ≈ 62.4 mm) and planar faces, which interlock adjacent staves. Encircling the stave assembly are four annular hoop rings in two size classes: two larger rings (~188.5 mm outer diameter, cd8a9240) and two smaller rings (~163 mm outer diameter, cd8a443a), each with sculpted toroidal-conical-B-spline cross-sections and no flat faces. Two disc caps (~141.2 mm diameter, 12 mm thick) close the barrel ends — one plain (cd8a1ce4, conical chamfer and toroidal blend rim) and one decorated (cd8784dc, 44 B-spline surfaces forming embossed script, plus a 10 mm-radius cylindrical locating boss 3 mm deep). A flanged collar (cd8b5590, ~40 mm flange diameter, 15 mm tall, with a countersunk through-hole of 23 mm mouth / 15 mm bore diameter) sits atop the barrel as a bung fitting, capped by a simple solid cylindrical plug (cd8ba3a8, R 16.25 mm, 14 mm tall). The tap sub-assembly on the front face consists of a dual-lobe knob (cd86c1b6, ~30.5 × 32 × 45 mm, blind bore R 4 mm, depth ~27.5 mm), a ball-lever handle (cd870fc2, sphere with a 5 mm-diameter lever arm and a disc base R ≈ 10.6 mm), and a spacer sleeve (cd8736da, outer R 4 mm, inner R 3 mm, 40 mm long, 6 mm through-hole). The cradle comprises two saddle supports (cd7e5d26 and cd7fe3e4, each ~170 × 45 × 25 mm, with concave semicylindrical channels R 12.5 mm forming a 2-member mirror pair per the mirror_pair layout tag), two prismatic crossbars (~25 × 25 × 220 mm each, one structural bar cd814340, one spacer bar cd8206c2 with raised pads), and a trapezoidal-section key bar (cd82ca18, ~25 × 25 × 189 mm, 22° angled sides). No contact edges or hole-edge links were recorded in the assembly graph, so all interface inferences rest on geometric compatibility alone.

Parts:
- curved_segment (×18): A gently arched, slender bar roughly 260 mm long with a near-square cross-section of about 33 mm, giving an aspect ratio of ~7.9. Its surfaces are predominantly freeform B-spline (46 faces), with fine 0.1 mm-radius edge fillets (12 cylindrical faces) and toroidal blends. Stepped notch features at both ends, defined by six planar faces and two short cylindrical patches (R ≈ 62.4 mm), appear to serve as registration interfaces. With 18 instances in the assembly and no through-holes, this part likely functions as a repeating curved segment that tiles with identical copies to form a larger curved or annular structure.
- seal_ring (×2): A thin annular ring approximately 163 mm in diameter and 21 mm in axial height (aspect ratio ≈ 7.7), composed entirely of toroidal, conical, and B-spline surfaces with no flat or purely cylindrical faces, giving it a sculpted cross-sectional profile. Two straight-line edges mark a visible seam on the band. With no holes, no planar mating faces, and two instances in the assembly, the ring appears to serve as a seal or retaining ring whose profiled cross-section could interface with a cylindrical bore or shaft.
- seal_ring (×2): A thin circular ring with an outer diameter of approximately 188.5 mm and an axial height of about 21.9 mm, yielding an aspect ratio of 8.608. Its cross-section is not a simple rectangle; instead it is composed of 4 toroidal, 2 conical, and 2 B-spline faces that define a sculpted profile with rounded blends and tapered sections, with no planar or cylindrical surfaces present. There are no holes and no recorded assembly contacts. Appearing twice in the assembly, this part likely serves as a seal ring or retention band whose profiled cross-section could permit press-fit seating or controlled deformation against a mating surface.
- cap: A thin circular disc approximately 141 mm in diameter and 12 mm thick (aspect ratio ≈ 11.8) with flat top and bottom faces and a chamfered-and-filleted rim formed by one conical and one toroidal surface. It contains no holes, no cylindrical surfaces, and records no contact pairs in the assembly. The simple featureless disc geometry with a beveled edge profile suggests this part likely serves as a cap or blanking disc that could seat against or into a cylindrical opening.
- cap: A flat circular disc approximately 141 mm in diameter and 12 mm thick (aspect ratio ≈ 11.8), featuring embossed cursive script text across its top face represented by 44 B-spline surfaces. The perimeter has a raised rim with rounded toroidal and conical transitions, and a small cylindrical boss (R 10 mm, 3 mm axial extent) on the rear face could serve as a locating spigot. No holes are present and no assembly contacts are recorded. The part likely functions as a decorative cap or badge that could press-fit into a receiving bore.
- flanged_collar: A squat, axially symmetric stepped collar approximately 40 mm in diameter and 15 mm tall (aspect ratio ≈ 2.67), featuring a wide base flange (R 20 mm), a narrow circumferential groove at R 16.25 mm, an upper hub at R 13.75 mm, and a central countersunk through-hole (23 mm countersink diameter narrowing to a 15 mm bore). Four toroidal fillets smooth the step transitions. The flat annular top and bottom faces and the through-bore suggest this part could serve as a flanged collar or bushing seat, likely permitting a shaft or fastener to pass through while bearing against an adjacent flat surface.

All long edges carry small 1.0 mm fillet rounds, and the hump ends terminate in spherical quarter-caps. No holes are present and no assembly contacts are recorded. The concave half-round trough appears to form a cradle that could locate a cylindrical object of roughly 25 mm diameter.

Both ends feature rounded dome-like caps formed by spherical and toroidal blends, while small 1.0 mm-radius fillets smooth the edges. A flat bottom face permits seating on a planar surface. With no holes and no recorded assembly contacts, the part appears to serve as a passive cradle or saddle support that could locate a cylindrical element of roughly 25 mm diameter.

All edges carry uniform 1.0 mm radius fillets, producing 59 cylindrical, 14 toroidal, and 24 spherical blend faces. Twenty-two planar faces span all six principal directions, providing flat mating surfaces on every side. No holes or recorded assembly contacts are present. Its cross-section has horizontal top and bottom faces and two symmetrically angled side faces inclined about 22° from vertical, with all twelve edges carrying 1.0 mm radius fillets and all eight corners blended with spherical surfaces. The flanged collar's countersunk hole (23 mm / 15 mm bore) and the spacer sleeve's 6 mm through-hole appear in part dossiers but have no matching fastener bodies in the hole_edges_summary.
items.judge_reason: PRED and GT both represent a barrel on a cradle with matching overall proportions and main features. However, there are noticeable geometric differences: 1) The cradle structure in PRED is misarranged, featuring three layers with the bottom supports running perpendicular to the barrel and having chamfered ends, whereas GT has two parallel crossbars and a central key bar; 2) The saddle supports in PRED have flat, blocky ends, while GT's have rounded, humped ends; 3) The embossed 'Debowa' text on the front cap is a standard sans-serif font in PRED, compared to cursive script in GT; 4) The tap assembly in PRED lacks the specific lever arm detail seen in GT. PRED is otherwise very clean and well-detailed.

gt.name: curved arc segment
gt.semantic: A slender, gently curved bar about 259.6 mm long with a 32–33 mm cross-section (aspect ratio ~7.88), whose flanks are mostly freeform BSpline swept surfaces following a sweep radius of roughly 62.39 mm. Twelve tiny 0.1 mm cylindrical faces and twelve toroidal faces indicate the long edges are uniformly fillet-rounded, while only 6 small planar faces (normals ±Z and the symmetric pair 0.174, ±0.985, 0) form stepped, angled shoulders at each tip. No holes and no recorded contact pairs are present, so the only connection-relevant features are these notched end shoulders. With 18 instances in the assembly, the part likely serves as one arc segment that could butt-join end-to-end with its siblings to form a larger curved ring or frame.

gt.name: ring
gt.semantic: A thin, large-diameter circular ring with bbox 188.53 × 188.53 × 21.90 mm and an aspect_ratio of 8.608, i.e. a flat hoop whose diameter is roughly 8.6× its axial height. Its 8 faces decompose into 4 toroidal rim blends, 2 slightly conical inner/outer walls, and 2 small B-spline patches closing a seam (with 14 circular edges and 2 line edges), and the silhouette in the image shows a smooth rounded band with a faint vertical seam. There are no holes and no recorded contact pairs, so interfacing is inferred from geometry only: the inner conical surface could seat coaxially on a cylindrical shoulder while the toroidal rims could bear axially against flat shoulders. The part likely serves as a retaining or spacing ring that could be captured coaxially between two larger circular features.

gt.name: ring
gt.semantic: A thin circular ring approximately 163 mm in outer span and 21 mm thick (aspect_ratio 7.694), built from 2 conical flanks, 4 toroidal rim blends, and 2 small B-spline seam patches, with 14 circular edges and no holes or planar faces. The slightly tapered cones and rounded toroidal edges suggest a ring that could nest coaxially in a circular bore or recess. It likely serves as a ring/spacer-type element that permits coaxial seating against a mating circular feature.

gt.name: disc cap
gt.semantic: A thin solid disc roughly 141 mm in diameter and 12 mm thick (aspect_ratio 11.77), bounded by two large parallel planar decks with normals along ±Z and a rim composed of a narrow flat band, a conical chamfer, and a toroidal blend (3 planes + 1 cone + 1 torus, 5 circular edges). It carries no holes and no recorded assembly contacts, leaving only the two flat faces and the blended rim as potential interfaces. It likely functions as a cover, blank cap, or spacer disc that could seat flat against a mating planar surface, with the chamfered edge permitting entry into a circular recess.

gt.name: stepped bushing
gt.semantic: A short axisymmetric stepped bushing roughly 40 × 40 × 15 mm (aspect_ratio 2.667) formed from four concentric cylindrical steps at radii 20.0, 16.25, 13.75 and 7.5 mm, with toroidal blends between steps and a conical countersink leading into the 7.5 mm central bore that runs ~11 mm along the Y axis. Two flat ±Y annular faces bound the flange, and a small 2.3 mm countersunk through-hole sits on the −Y face. The stepped outer diameters could nest into a matching counterbored seat while the central bore permits a shaft or pin to pass through. It likely serves as a stepped bushing or bore-reducing spacer.

gt.name: cap
gt.semantic: A short circular cap roughly 32.5 mm in diameter and 14 mm tall (aspect ratio 2.321), built from one cylindrical side wall of radius 16.25 mm, two parallel planar faces forming the top and bottom (normals along ±Y), and two toroidal blends rounding the upper edge. It has no through-holes and no recorded assembly contacts. The flat underside and the 16.25 mm cylindrical wall could seat against or nest into a matching bore, suggesting this part likely functions as a closure cap.

gt.name: tubular sleeve
gt.semantic: A slender hollow tube roughly 8 mm in outer diameter and 40 mm long (aspect_ratio 5.0), with an outer cylindrical wall of radius 4.0 mm, a full-length inner bore of radius 3.0 mm, two flat annular end faces with opposed normals along ±Y, and a small cone+torus chamfer at one end. A 0.6 mm diameter radial through-hole pierces the wall near one end. The Ø6 through-bore permits a shaft or pin to pass the full length, while the outer Ø8 surface and end annuli could seat into a mating bore and against a flat shoulder. The geometry suggests it likely functions as a thin-walled sleeve or bushing/spacer, with the small cross-hole possibly accommodating a retention pin.

gt.name: bar spacer
gt.semantic: A slender square-section bar, 25 × 25 × 220 mm (aspect_ratio 8.8), with all external edges broken by a uniform 1 mm round (60 cylindrical, 16 toroidal, and 24 spherical faces blending into 22 planar faces). Two shallow stepped reliefs near each end create a slightly raised central deck between two square end blocks. There are no holes and no recorded contact pairs, leaving the four long flat sides and two square end faces (normals along ±X, ±Y, ±Z) as the candidate mating surfaces. The geometry appears consistent with a spacer or filler bar that could seat flat against a counterface, with the end steps likely permitting nesting into a recess.

gt.name: chamfered bar
gt.semantic: A slender prismatic bar roughly 25 × 25 × 189 (aspect_ratio 7.562) with a flat bottom, two symmetric sloped top facets meeting along a central ridge, and chamfered ends, giving a roof-like profile. All edges are uniformly rounded with a single fillet radius of 1.0 (12 cylindrical faces) blended at the corners by 8 small spherical patches, while 6 planar faces form the main facets. There are no holes and no recorded assembly contacts, so the flat underside and end planes are the only obvious candidates that could seat against mating surfaces. It appears to likely act as a passive bar, wedge, or key-like element.

gt.name: saddle block
gt.semantic: An elongated bar-like block of roughly 170 × 45 × 25 mm (aspect_ratio 6.8) with a concave cylindrical trough of radius 12.5 mm scooped along its top between two raised, rounded end lobes, formed by 2 large cylinder faces over ~90 mm of axial extent and blended by 4 spherical and 4 toroidal corner faces plus numerous 1.0 mm edge fillets. Five planar faces match the five axis-aligned plane normals, giving a flat bottom, flat back, two end faces, and small flat tops on the lobes. With no holes and no recorded assembly contacts, the salient interface features are the concave 12.5 mm cradle surface on top and the flat bottom/back planes. The geometry suggests it likely serves as a saddle or cradle support that nests a cylindrical shaft of ~25 mm diameter while seating on its flat underside.
